questions.question: What is the main website URL where you access NotebookLM?
choices: notebooklm.google.com
google.notebooklm.com
notebooks.google.com
ai.google.com/notebook
questions.feedback_correct: Exactly right—notebooklm.google.com is the address where you sign in with your Google account.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The correct address is notebooklm.google.com. This is the direct link provided on Slide 6 where you begin the exercise.

questions.question: What is Studio in NotebookLM?
choices: A web browser extension for faster searching
The panel inside every notebook where you generate rich content formats like Audio and Video Overviews
A separate subscription service for video editing
A public gallery where you can view other users' notebooks
questions.feedback_correct: That's correct—Studio is the content generation panel where you turn your sources into formats like Audio Overviews and Video Overviews.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Studio is not a browser tool, subscription service, or public gallery. It's the content generation panel built into every NotebookLM notebook, where you access Audio and Video Overviews.

questions.question: What does NotebookLM do when you ask it a question?
choices: It searches the open web and pulls in outside context from multiple sources
It uses only the sources you have given it—no web searches, no hallucinated context
It connects to other users' notebooks to find related information
It retrieves information from Google's main search index
questions.feedback_correct: Correct—NotebookLM works exclusively from the sources you provide, which is a core feature that keeps outputs grounded and traceable.
questions.feedback_incorrect: NotebookLM does not browse the web or pull in outside context. It works only with the material you give it, which ensures every insight can be traced back to your actual sources.

questions.question: What happens after you add a website source to your notebook?
choices: The source is instantly ready to use with no processing time
NotebookLM reads, indexes, and processes the page, then displays the title and summary in the Sources list when complete
You must manually summarize the content before it can be used
The source is added but remains hidden until you generate content from it
questions.feedback_correct: Exactly—NotebookLM processes the page and shows you confirmation by displaying the title and summary in the Sources panel.
questions.feedback_incorrect: After you add a source, NotebookLM processes it automatically. You'll know it's ready when the title and summary appear in your Sources list on the left side of the screen.

questions.question: What is a Video Overview in NotebookLM?
choices: A raw video recording of the webpage content
A structured, presenter-style summary built directly from your source material, similar to a briefing
A slideshow with images extracted from the webpage
An interactive tool where you can edit the source material in real time
questions.feedback_correct: That's right—a Video Overview is a structured, presenter-style briefing synthesized from your sources, designed for quick understanding.
questions.feedback_incorrect: A Video Overview is not a raw recording, slideshow, or editing tool. It's a structured summary that presents your source material in a presenter-style format, like a briefing you'd watch before a meeting.

questions.question: How long does the hands-on exercise portion of this course take to complete?
choices: About 5 minutes
About 30 minutes total for the whole session
Roughly 16 minutes for the hands-on portion
About 70 seconds
questions.feedback_correct: Correct—the instructor notes indicate the hands-on portion takes roughly 16 minutes, which is separate from the full 30-minute session.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The full session is about 30 minutes, but the hands-on exercise itself is roughly 16 minutes. The 70 seconds mentioned is just the interface video preview.

questions.question: What can you do at the end of the hands-on exercise to help you repeat this process later?
choices: Ask the course to produce a written step-by-step summary document you can keep and reuse
Automatically receive an email with the steps
Download a pre-made PDF template from the course resources
Record a video of yourself completing the steps
questions.feedback_correct: Perfect—you can ask for a written summary of every step you completed, which becomes a reference document you can use anytime.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The course offers a written summary document that you can request using a specific prompt. This becomes a clean, reusable reference you can keep for future use.

questions.question: Why is NotebookLM's approach of working only from your own sources described as a feature rather than a limitation?
choices: It prevents the tool from being too powerful and overwhelming
It ensures the output is grounded in your actual material and every insight can be traced back to something you provided
It makes the tool easier for beginners to understand
It reduces the cost of running the service
questions.feedback_correct: Exactly—by limiting outputs to only your sources, NotebookLM ensures accountability and traceability, which is a strength, not a weakness.
questions.feedback_incorrect: This approach is a feature because it keeps outputs grounded and verifiable. You can always trace where an insight came from, since it must come from sources you actually provided.

questions.question: Based on the course, when would NotebookLM be most useful to you?
choices: Only when you need to generate creative fiction content
When you bring it your own material like a PDF report, long article, or set of notes you actually need to work with
When you want to browse the latest news and trends from across the web
When you need to verify information from multiple internet sources
questions.feedback_correct: That's right—NotebookLM earns its usefulness by working with your actual material, not by being a general search or research tool.
questions.feedback_incorrect: NotebookLM is most valuable when you load your own material—reports, articles, notes—that you genuinely need to understand or process. It's designed for working with content you already have, not for general web browsing.
